title: Presentation-trait style vocabulary — the intents-parallel style axis (name, scope, meta-schema)
summary: Grounds decision #1884: mint the style sibling of intents — a declarative OPEN vocabulary for the presentation/surface axis (rounded, shadowed, bordered, hover…), each resolving to one CSS property or a multi-prop recipe, with declared interaction logic between traits. Surfaced by the we-card docs dogfood (#1871/#1608): reducing bespoke .section-card CSS to 'native element + consumed standard' exposed that WE has a first-class open UX vocabulary (intents, standardized meta-schema, UX-only by statute) but NO standard for the style axis — only theme tokens (single values, one-way-projected from the JS-first injector). So the Plateau assembler papers the gap with hand-authored CSS blobs (we:src/_data/assemblerPresets/hovercard.json:68-70 ships a literal .hovercard-card { border-radius; box-shadow; transition }), which is component-implementation leaking into products. Three-tier model to validate: tokens (primitive + DTCG composite types) → presentation traits (the new open-vocab+meta-schema standard, WE owns contract type-only, FUI owns the recipe→CSS engine) → flavors/recipes (the assembler composes these). Web prior-art survey harvests four paradigms and is decisive on two reshapes. PARADIGMS: (A) Tailwind utilities = one-utility=one-declaration tied to a named token scale (rounded-sm/md/lg/full) with orthogonal composing modifier-prefixes (hover:/md:/dark:); Chakra style-props = explicit prop→CSS-property map resolving through the theme token scale, with _hover/_focus pseudo-state props — together the tier1→tier2 seam. (B) Open Props = the primitive-values layer, just custom properties (--radius-2/--shadow-3), confirming tier1 is values-only and traits reference token names not inline values. (C) the recipe/variant engines — Panda, vanilla-extract, Stitches — all converge on the SAME four-key shape { base, variants, compoundVariants, defaultVariants }, where COMPOUND VARIANTS (extra CSS only when multiple variant dimensions intersect, 'only the matching combo merges') ARE the declared inter-trait interaction the decision needs (rounded clips hover=shine; elevated⟹shadow+bg); a flavor IS a config-recipe, a trait IS one named variant dimension, an interaction IS one compoundVariant entry. (D) DTCG composite token types (shadow/border/typography/transition/gradient, each grouping sub-props) ground the '1 prop vs recipe' duality. (E) ARIA/MDN have NO presentational-appearance vocabulary — aria-disabled is semantic-state-only and explicitly leaves styling a separate developer concern; role=presentation removes semantics; CSS appearance only controls native-widget rendering and 'does not affect non-widgets' — so the platform itself separates semantic-state from appearance, the strongest defense that finishes stay separate from intents/UX. RESHAPES: the survey DISSOLVES a sub-fork ('1 CSS prop vs multi-prop recipe?' is not an authoring choice — it falls out of whether the trait targets a primitive property or a DTCG composite token; recipe-ness is derived) and ADDS a scope sub-boundary (the action-emphasis precedent shows UX-meaning treatment selectors like button emphasis fill|outline|ghost stay as intent dimensions on their owner — so fork 2 is three-way: intents/UX-treatment ⟂ finishes/surface-physics ⟂ layout/spacing, not the posed two-way). Three forks at DoR: (1) NAME = `finishes` (material-finish metaphor, pairs with flavors, no collision with the droplist `traits`/webtraits project or the `surface` intent or faceted-search; over facets/aspects/surfaces/treatments); (2) SCOPE = surface appearance only (shape/border/elevation/gloss/interaction-feedback); spacing routes OUT to the layout/region system; UX-meaning treatments stay on intents; (3) META-SCHEMA = a finish declares { target (CSS prop OR DTCG composite), tokenFamily, scale, default, states, interactions[] } and the FUI resolver composes a set by declared cascade order, last-wins conflict precedence, compoundVariant-style interactions, pseudo-state nesting, and responsive value-as-breakpoint-object — adopting the industry-converged recipe four-key shape rather than coining a novel one. Home relatedProject:webtheme (already owns the DTCG→native-CSS token layer, #364). Does NOT block the card — the card-surface is just one recipe and can ship on plain tokens now, retrofit later. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-27-presentation-trait-style-vocabulary.md.
